_comment: external-agents config. The caller picks ONE effort level (low|medium|high|xhigh) and each agent's tier maps it to the right (model, native effort). 'enabled' agents run under `--agent all`. With no --effort, default_tier is used. agy and cursor bake the tier into the model name and ignore 'effort'; codex/claude take model + effort separately. The cursor agent's CLI binary is `cursor-agent`; run `cursor-agent models` (once logged in) for its exact --model identifiers. NOTE: cursor tiers deliberately use NON-fast, ZDR-respecting model ids (no `-fast` suffix; gpt-5-mini/composer-2.5/gpt-5.5-high/claude-opus-4-8-thinking-high for low/medium/high/xhigh) — Cursor's own CLI default is a `-fast` variant (e.g. `composer-2.5-fast`) that spends fast/priority requests; keep the plain ids to avoid burning fast requests. Avoid the `claude-fable-5-*` ids (marked NO ZDR). agy tiers may add a 'fallback' model: the (limited) 3rd-party primary (e.g. Claude Opus 4.6 (Thinking)) runs only when `antigravity-usage --json` confirms remaining quota — otherwise (exhausted, or the Antigravity IDE is closed / not logged in) the larger-limit Gemini 'fallback' runs instead. agy-only; needs the Antigravity IDE open or `antigravity-usage login`. Read by scripts/run-agent.sh via jq (preferred) or python3. See README.md.
